title: Struthiopteris fallax
intro: Struthiopteris fallax, also known as the Australian water fern or struthiopteris, is a type of aquatic fern native to Australia and New Zealand. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting respiratory health. The bioactive constituents of Struthiopteris fallax include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Struthiopteris fallax is often prepared as a decoction or infusate, and is used to treat conditions such as bronchitis, coughs, and skin irritations.
taxonomy: Struthiopteris fallax belongs to the class Polypodiopsida, which includes a large group of vascular plants often referred to as ferns. This species specifically falls under the family Thelypteridaceae, which comprises the net-veined or net-leaved ferns. Struthiopteris fallax is further classified as Thelypteris fallax but is more commonly referred to as Struthiopteris fallax, due to the genus being revised. In terms of its order, Struthiopteris fallax is classified as Pteridales, but this order is sometimes combined with the order Polypodiales. This species is also known as a member of the division Pteridophyta, which comprises all vascular plants that reproduce by spores.
